task: I’m trying to put together a really simple morning routine that starts with a quick math refresher and then shifts into beginner yoga I can actually keep using. Please start on Google and search for a video lesson that clearly teaches both explicit and recursive formulas for arithmetic sequences, then open the actual video page and note the title, creator or channel, and URL so I have a study piece to come back to; if it looks solid, leave that tab open for me. After that, go to YouTube and find a morning yoga video that’s right around 20 minutes long, open the actual video page, and tell me the title and duration so I can see whether it feels short enough for a real weekday routine. If that general option seems reasonable, stay on YouTube and specifically check whether Yoga With Adriene has a morning yoga video under 20 minutes that would fit the same need, and confirm from the video page or description whether it’s actually a vinyasa flow style session; please open that in its own tab too so I can compare the two yoga options side by side. To round this out, use Google to find at least three approachable or funny yoga instructors who post free classes on YouTube, and for each one give me the instructor’s name, a channel or website link, and a short note on why they seem especially beginner-friendly compared with the yoga videos you found earlier. In the end, I just want a concise resource list for this morning routine, with the key links, and please keep the math video plus the two yoga video tabs open so I can look at them myself.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent successfully used Google to search for a video lesson on arithmetic sequences (explicit and recursive formulas). It identified and opened the video "Arithmetic Sequences: Writing Recursive & Explicit Formulas" by the channel "SimpLEEfied" (URL: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5aPQpVwN_Hc). The agent recorded the title, creator, and URL in its final summary, and the video remained open in a tab at the end of the session.

R2: success - The agent successfully searched for and opened a YouTube video titled "20 minute Morning Yoga Stretch | Sarah Beth Yoga" (URL: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TL_3LpGT4DI). The agent confirmed in step 33 that this tab was open. The video title is clearly visible in the tab title and the page content, and the duration is approximately 20 minutes (the agent noted 19:59 in step 10 and 1200 seconds in step 31). The requirement to open a YouTube morning yoga video of approximately 20 minutes and provide its title and duration is satisfied.

R3: success - The agent successfully opened a Yoga With Adriene video ("Short Wake Up Flow - 15 Minute Morning Yoga") in its own tab (tab index 1). The agent explicitly addressed the vinyasa determination in its final response, noting that while the video is a "flow," it does not explicitly label itself as "vinyasa" on the page, and it provided an alternative recommendation ("Yoga Joy | 20-Minute Full Body Vinyasa Flow") that is explicitly labeled as vinyasa. This demonstrates that the agent performed the required check and provided a supported determination.

R4: success - The agent successfully identified three distinct yoga instructors (Bad Yogi/Erin Motz, Yoga With Tim/Tim Senesi, and Yoga with Kassandra/Kassandra Reinhardt) via Google searches. For each instructor, the agent provided the name, a valid YouTube channel link, and a brief rationale explaining why they are beginner-friendly and how they compare to the previously found yoga videos (e.g., noting that Bad Yogi is more casual/irreverent, Tim is more alignment-focused, and Kassandra offers shorter, prop-free options). The requirements of the rubric are fully met.
